items.title: India withdraws bid to host COP33 climate summit in 2028
items.summary: The move comes amid global climate negotiations and rotating hosting responsibilities among UN regional groups

items.title: Gold Steadies as Traders Weigh Fragile Ceasefire in Iran War
items.summary: Gold steadied after a two-day gain as traders weighed the prospect for a diplomatic resolution to the Iran war, even as sporadic fighting threatened to derail a fragile ceasefire.

items.title: West Asia conflict deals a double whammy to India’s farm sector: pre-harvest cost pressures, post-harvest shipping delays
items.summary: Supply shocks, export disruptions, and labour shortages caused by the Iran war are beginning to strain India’s agriculture despite ample foodgrain stocks.

items.title: Status quo stance reflects prudent, calibrated approach: Setty
items.summary: “With growth projected at 6.9% for FY27 and inflation expected to remain within the target band, the policy underscores stability while leveraging communication and regulatory refinement as key levers,” C.S.Setty, chairman, State Bank of India  said

items.title: Korean Stocks, Won Surge as Iran Ceasefire Spurs Relief Rally
items.summary: South Korean assets surged after a last-minute temporary truce in the six-week old war in Iran raised hopes of easing energy supply disruptions, lifting risk appetite.

items.title: Oil Rises After Biggest Drop Since 2020 as Hormuz Stays Blocked
items.summary: Oil rebounded after its biggest one-day drop since April 2020, as the Strait of Hormuz remained largely blocked and Israeli attacks on Lebanon threatened to derail the fragile ceasefire in the Middle East.

items.title: IGoM reviews preparedness amid West Asia developments, ensures energy, food security
items.summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh says government is ensuring uninterrupted availability of LPG, petrol, diesel and fertilizers, while maintaining supplies of essential commodities

items.title: Shippers seek clarity as Iran still controls Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Indian vessels await government clearance; more than 25 India-bound ships still stuck

items.title: RBI simplifies TReDS onboarding for MSMEs
items.summary: The scope of TReDS was further expanded in 2023 with the inclusion of insurance companies as the fourth participant

items.title: RBI monetary policy: Currency forward curbs temporary, says Sanjay Malhotra
items.summary: "These measures are not in any sense signalling any structural change. We stand committed to the development, broadening and deepening of these markets and the internationalisation of rupee. So, obviously these measures are not going to remain there forever," governor Sanjay Malhotra said.

items.title: Hotels bet on quick Middle East recovery post ceasefire
items.summary: The hospitality sector anticipates a strong recovery in the Middle East after the US-Iran ceasefire. Industry leaders expect occupancies in markets like Dubai to bounce back by September. While rates may take longer to reach previous levels, the ceasefire is seen as positive news for tourism and energy markets. Airline flight frequencies will play a key role in the rebound.

items.title: BEML, DMRC join hands to bid for metro projects in West Asia
items.summary: BEML Limited and Delhi Metro Rail Corporation Limited are joining forces. They will jointly bid for metro rail projects in West Asia. This partnership will allow them to offer complete services. This includes supplying rolling stock. The two public sector entities have identified upcoming projects. This collaboration marks a significant step for both organizations.

items.title: US stocks today: Dow Jones surges 1,300 points, S&P, Nasdaq 2% on U.S.-Iran ceasefire
items.summary: World markets experienced a dramatic uplift as the U.S. and Iran cemented a two-week ceasefire, alleviating concerns over inflation and volatile oil markets. With this breakthrough, major U.S. stock indices soared, especially in sectors tied to economic growth. European markets and the global index mirrored this positive trend. In the wake of the announcement, oil prices took a notable downturn.

items.title: RBI monetary policy: Call below repo to comfort banks
items.summary: "Our attempt, of course, is to keep the WACR as near as possible to the policy rate. However, in times like this when there is so much uncertainty, we want to give comfort to banks that liquidity will not be in deficit. So that's why we have allowed WACR to be at the lower end of the LAF corridor," RBI governor Sanjay Malhotra said in his post-policy press conference.

items.title: MNRE to review solar project delays ahead of ALMM rollout
items.summary: The ministry of new and renewable energy will hold a review meeting with manufacturers and developers over concerns about delays in project commissioning ahead of the implementation of the approved list of models and manufacturers (ALMM) for solar cells from June, secretary Santosh Sarangi said.

items.title: PM Surya Ghar Yojana: 8 installations per minute in FY26 as India clinches record growth in renewables
items.summary: India’s renewable energy sector recorded significant growth in 2025-26, with rapid capacity additions across solar and wind segments, MNRE Pralhad Joshi said on Wednesday, April 8. Addressing a press conference, the minister said the country’s renewable energy capacity has nearly tripled over the past 11 years to around 275 GW.

items.title: NTPC, EDF sign pact to explore nuclear projects in India
items.summary: NTPC Ltd has forged a promising partnership with Electricite de France (EDF) to investigate innovative nuclear power ventures in India. This collaboration focuses on assessing the suitability of EDF's EPR technology, following recent government endorsements.

items.title: SEZs seek duty relief for three years, higher domestic sales limit
items.summary: Special Economic Zone developers and units are urging the government for extended domestic sales concessions. They want a three-year period instead of one year. A higher cap of 50% on domestic sales is also sought, up from the current 30%.

items.title: Tiruppur garment orders drop 15% as US, Europe demand weakens amid Iran War
items.summary: Tiruppur's apparel exports faced a 15% drop in March from the US and Europe. Buyers there held existing stock amid inflation concerns. Demand from West Asia halted due to the Iran war. This disrupted trade routes and increased costs. Manufacturers are also seeing rising input prices.

items.title: Centre to roll out PM-KUSUM 2, push rooftop solar to hit 10 million homes
items.summary: India is set to boost solar power with new schemes. The PM-KUSUM 2 initiative will expand solar irrigation. The PM Suryaghar Muft Bijli Yojana aims to connect 10 million homes to solar power. Rooftop solar installations are progressing rapidly. Efforts are underway to address pending power sale agreements and transmission challenges to ensure green energy distribution.

items.title: Oil tumbles below $100 after Trump announces two-week ceasefire
items.summary: GLOBAL-OIL:Oil tumbles below $100 after Trump announces two-week ceasefire

items.title: Mudra loans cross Rs 40 lakh crore, Nirmala Sitharaman hails MSME credit push
items.summary: The Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana has empowered millions of entrepreneurs. Over 577.9 million loans totaling ₹40.07 lakh crore have been disbursed since its 2015 launch. A significant portion of these loans went to women entrepreneurs, fostering self-reliance. The scheme has democratized access to credit, removing barriers for many individuals previously excluded from formal banking systems.

items.title: Govt moves to cushion jet fuel spike with 25% airport charge cut
items.summary: Airlines will see a 25% reduction in aircraft landing and parking charges for domestic flights. This relief measure will be in effect for three months. The government expects airlines to save approximately ₹400 crore during this period.

items.title: NTPC inks MoU with Électricité de France for cooperation in nuclear power generation
items.summary: NTPC intends to have 30GW nuclear capacity by 2047

items.title: Piyush Goyal urges exporters to tap India-US portal to boost trade
items.summary: India and the US are pushing businesses to use a new trade facilitation portal. This initiative aims to significantly increase bilateral trade, with both nations targeting $500 billion. The portal is designed to connect businesses across various sectors, from agriculture to technology. This development is expected to create new opportunities and strengthen economic ties between the two countries.

items.title: Short Sellers Squeezed Hard as Avis Shares Rocket 150%
items.summary: A highly shorted stock. A shrinking pool of shares available to trade. A price that keeps climbing.

items.title: Middle East rebuild opportunity emerges after ceasefire; Indian EPC stocks rally
items.summary: A ceasefire between the United States and Iran offers hope for Middle East peace and rebuilding. Indian construction and EPC companies are poised to benefit from massive reconstruction projects. Companies like L&T, KPIL, and KEC International have significant exposure to the region. Damage to energy assets across nine countries will require extensive repairs and new infrastructure development.

items.title: Banks Selling $3 Billion Debt for Meta’s Prometheus Data Center
items.summary: A group of banks including Natixis SA, Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group Inc. and Societe Generale SA have started selling $3 billion of loans for a data center in Ohio backed by Meta Platforms Inc., the latest deal supporting the buildout of artificial intelligence infrastructure.

items.title: FIFA cuts India media rights price to $35 million, still no takers for World Cups
items.summary: FIFA is struggling to sell media rights for the 2026 and 2030 World Cups in India. The governing body has significantly reduced its asking price. This situation arises from a slowdown in the sports broadcasting market and fewer potential buyers. JioStar, the current rights holder, is seen as the main contender.

items.title: Cabinet clears Rs 41,534 crore fertiliser subsidy for kharif 2026
items.summary: The Union cabinet has approved nutrient-based subsidy rates for phosphatic and potassic fertilisers for the upcoming kharif 2026 season. An allocation of Rs 41,534 crore has been made to support farmers. This move aims to insulate farmers from global price volatility and ensure adequate fertiliser availability. The government is committed to maintaining a steady supply of fertilisers at reasonable prices.

items.title: Treasuries Waver as Iranian Official Says Ceasefire Violated
items.summary: Treasuries wavered between gains and losses, erasing most of an earlier rally, after an Iranian official said the ceasefire deal with the US had been violated.

items.title: Oil prices plunge, global stocks surge as Trump announces Iran ceasefire
items.summary: Global stock markets soared and oil prices dropped significantly on Wednesday. This followed President Donald Trump's announcement of a ceasefire with Iran. The S&P 500, Dow Jones Industrial Average, and Nasdaq composite all saw substantial gains. European and Asian markets also experienced larger increases. The development eased tensions regarding the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Centre to soon set up a digital portal to track narcotic drugs
items.summary: The proposed platform will enable real-time, end-to-end digital tracking of all regulated pharmaceutical products, covering every stage from manufacturing or import through distribution, stock management and sale.

items.title: RBI holds repo rate, flags supply chain risks to inflation & growth
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India maintained its key policy rate at 5.25% due to ongoing West Asia tensions, warning of supply chain disruptions impacting inflation and growth. Projections for GDP and inflation remain within targets, though geopolitical uncertainty poses downside risks. Markets reacted positively to the news.

items.title: Cabinet Committee hikes HPCL Rajasthan Refinery project cost by 84% to ₹79,459 crore
items.summary: Hindustan Petroleum would be infusing ₹8,962 crore of additional equity investment.

items.title: Daikin Industries to invest Rs 1,000 crore in India for global R&D centre
items.summary: Daikin Industries is set to channel a monumental one thousand crore rupees into India to launch its first global R&D hub beyond Japan. This innovative center will specialize in software and product developments tailored for cooling systems.

items.title: The thermal cost of India’s textile surge
items.summary: The productivity crisis is no longer a theoretical risk; it is a mechanical and biological reality crippling India’s industrial heartlands

items.title: Fed minutes show growing openness to rate hikes at March meeting
items.summary: In recent meetings, Federal Reserve officials mulled the idea of increasing interest rates as inflation rates climbed beyond target thresholds, influenced in part by the intensifying U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran. Despite this, there is a strong expectation among many for rate cuts on the horizon.

items.title: US banks could release $320 billion in capital with new draft rules, analysts say
items.summary: USA-BANKS/CAPITAL:US banks could release $320 billion in capital with new draft rules, analysts say

items.title: Tata Motors eyes global expansion post demerger, aligns PV biz with JLR; Iveco deal to boost CV footprint
items.summary: Tata Motors is boosting its global presence after a successful FY26 and business split. The company is integrating its passenger car division with Jaguar Land Rover to find new efficiencies. Strong domestic sales in passenger and commercial vehicles are driving this growth. Tata Motors is also looking at acquiring Iveco to become a top global commercial vehicle maker.

items.title: Fed's Daly says US economic fundamentals in a 'good place'
items.summary: Mary Daly, the head of the San Francisco Federal Reserve, is optimistic about the vitality of the US economy. With consumers maintaining their spending habits and businesses showing a commitment to investment, she emphasizes resilience. Despite worries over inflation due to rising oil prices stemming from the Iran conflict, Daly reassures that the labor market is robust.

items.title: April’s First US Treasury Auctions See Improved Demand
items.summary: The first Treasury auctions of the month attracted improved investor demand, diluting concern that foreigners were avoiding US debt amid the war in the Middle East.

items.title: Bank NPAs decline to 2%, no systemic hit due to Middle East conflict: RBI
items.summary: Indian banks saw their bad loan ratio drop to 2% by December 2025. This improvement spans across retail, services, industry, and agriculture sectors. Bank credit growth accelerated to 13.8 percent year-on-year by March 2026. The Reserve Bank of India noted no systemic concerns despite global supply chain disruptions.

items.title: IBBI panel suggests project-wise insolvency resolution for real estate sector
items.summary: A panel suggested restricting real estate insolvency proceedings to stressed projects, not entire companies, to protect homebuyers in solvent projects. The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) panel also recommended raising the minimum default threshold to Rs 5 crore and developing project-wise lending frameworks.

items.title: Indian tech firms to forecast a diverging recovery in FY27 even as Accenture signals resilience: analysts
items.summary: Geopolitical disturbances – from U.S.-China tensions to European slowdowns – will keep tech consulting lumpy. But managed services will lead growth in FY27, predicts Avinash Vashistha, former Chairman of Accenture

items.title: Traders place large $950 million bet on oil price falling hours ahead of ceasefire
items.summary: Just as the US and Iran reached a crucial ceasefire, investors took a colossal leap into the market with a $950 million bet against rising oil prices. This gamble follows a notable trend of large trades in recent weeks.

items.title: IRDAI issues revised information and cyber security guidelines
Cyient Semiconductors acquires 74% stake in Kinetic Tech for $85 million
FedEx trucking spinoff targets 2026 operating margin of 12%
items.summary: As FedEx Freight prepares for its upcoming independence on June 1, it's setting ambitious goals with a projected 12 percent operating margin this year. The trucking giant forecasts a hefty revenue of $8.7 billion, alongside promising medium-term growth for its revenue and core profits. With a strong focus on modernization investments, the company aims to steadily improve its margins.

items.title: Fragile ceasefire broken? Fresh escalations reported hours after US-Iran truce
items.summary: Hours after both the US and Iran confirmed a two-week ceasefire in the sixth week of the Middle East conflict, Tehran claimed to have been struck by American forces with several other countries in the region in a complex web of fresh attacks on Wednesday, according to foreign media reports Israel

items.title: Why the bond market won't bounce back to pre-war levels
items.summary: In light of the recent ceasefire, the prospects for global bond markets remain fraught with difficulty. Energy prices continue to climb, accompanied by stubborn inflation that renders a smooth recovery uncertain. As central banks appear reluctant to lower interest rates this year, investors should gear up for an extended era of higher rates.

items.title: Amid elevated gold prices, Titan logs 46% revenue growth in Q4; jewellery sales rise 52%
items.summary: Titan Co Ltd reported a strong 46 percent revenue growth in the March quarter of FY26. The jewellery division, a major contributor, saw significant year-on-year expansion. This marks the second consecutive quarter of robust growth for the Tata Group firm.

items.title: Ex-Rio Tinto CEO's deep-sea mining firm to merge with Odyssey in $1 billion deal
items.summary: A transformative merger is on the horizon as American Ocean Minerals, spearheaded by Tom Albanese, partners with Odyssey Marine Exploration to unveil one of the largest collections of deep-sea mineral reserves. This ambitious endeavor focuses on tapping into essential minerals buried beneath the waves and will see the newly formed entity listed on the Nasdaq.

items.title: Lupin shares to be in focus on Thursday as USFDA approves Dapagliflozin and Metformin tablets
items.summary: Lupin's shares are expected to attract investor attention as the company received U.S. FDA approval for Dapagliflozin and Metformin Hydrochloride Extended-Release Tablets.&nbsp;

items.title: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri launches India-US trade Facilitation Portal
items.summary: India's Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri launched the India-US Trade Facilitation Portal. This initiative aims to strengthen existing supply chains and foster new commercial ties. The portal supports the goal of reaching $500 billion in bilateral trade. The launch occurred during Misri's visit to the US to discuss trade and defense relations.

items.title: Meta unveils first AI model from costly superintelligence team
items.summary: In a bold leap into the future, Meta Platforms has introduced Muse Spark, the first artificial intelligence model crafted by its advanced superintelligence team. This launch is a clear signal of Meta's intention to stand toe-to-toe with competitors in the accelerating AI arena. The company's substantial financial commitment to AI innovation aims to transform user engagement across its services.

items.title: NCLAT upholds NCLT order on distribution of funds from a resolution plan
items.summary: The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal has rejected a plea from five banks. They challenged fund distribution to SBI and Punjab National Bank. The tribunal upheld the distribution mechanism. This mechanism was approved by the lenders' body and the NCLT. The case involves OCL Iron and Steel insolvency proceedings. Indrani Patnaik's resolution plan was approved.

items.title: Delta Air Lines shares climb over 11% even as carrier forecasts higher fuel costs in second quarter
items.summary: Delta Air Lines anticipates more than $2 billion in additional fuel expenditures through June due to the ongoing Iran conflict, leading management to adopt a cautious stance and maintain its existing full year earnings target

items.title: Intel shares jump 9.5% to near 5-year high after company joins Elon Musk’s Terafab project
items.summary: Intel shares surged 9.5% on 8 April, reaching $57.92, continuing a six-session winning streak. The rise is fuelled by the company's partnership with Elon Musk's Terafab initiative, aiming to revolutionise semiconductor production and boost AI capabilities.

items.title: Iran shuts Strait of Hormuz amid Israel strikes on Lebanon, hours after ceasefire
items.summary: Tensions in the Gulf region have escalated dramatically as Iran closes the Strait of Hormuz, a key passage for energy shipments. This decision follows Israeli military actions in Lebanon, effectively blocking one of the world's most crucial energy routes.

items.title: RBI shuns hawkish signal, keeps rates unchanged amid war shock
items.summary: MPC holds repo rate at 5.25% and retains the neutral stance. RBI pegs FY26 growth higher at 7.6%, but with downside risks; says inflation is contained.

items.title: What lies ahead for Air India’s next CEO after Campbell Wilson
items.summary: In many ways, the runway has been laid, aircraft spruced up and the engines spooled. The take-off, however, will be under a new captain

items.title: Market records best day in 5 years as ceasefire hopes emerge
items.summary: Market rallies 4% as Sensex and Nifty achieve best gains in five years amid ceasefire hopes and falling crude prices

items.title: Bosh to acquire its vehicle motion business Bosh Chassis Systems India
items.summary: Bosch Limited plans to acquire its vehicle motion business, Bosch Chassis Systems India. This move will create a stronger mobility portfolio for the company. The acquisition aims to enhance Bosch's capabilities in safety and braking systems. It will also allow for further investment in these critical areas. The transaction is subject to shareholder approval.

items.title: Trai weighs direct satellite-to-mobile communication services
items.summary: India's telecom regulator Trai is exploring direct satellite communication to mobile phones. This aims to provide seamless connectivity across rural and remote areas. Trai is seeking public views on using spectrum for this Direct-to-Device service. The move could bring satellite services on par with mobile providers. Decisions on spectrum usage are pending international conferences.

items.title: Tata Trusts trustee Vijay Singh received over ₹20 crore as commission and fees from Tata companies
items.summary: Singh’s fees as a nominee director on the boards of the companies should have gone to the charitable trusts, if good governance practices were followed

items.title: Cabinet approves two hydro-electric projects in Arunachal Pradesh with a total outlay exceeding ₹40,000 crore
items.summary: Cabinet approved proposal for construction of two major hydro-power electric projects, that is Kamala Hydro Electric project and the Kalai-II Hydro-electric project.

items.title: RBI MPC keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25%
items.summary: Intensity and duration of West Asia conflict, damage to energy and other infrastructure add risk to inflation and growth outlooks, says panel, cuts growth forecast to 6.9%, hikes inflation projection to 4.5%

items.title: Railways account for 28-30% of freight movement, well below global benchmarks: ASSOCHAM
items.summary: Indian Railways is transforming to boost freight movement. The sector aims for 3,000 million tonnes capacity by 2030. Initiatives like Dedicated Freight Corridors and electrification are enhancing efficiency. Increasing rail's modal share is crucial for reducing logistics costs and strengthening global competitiveness. This transformation positions Indian Railways as a key driver of economic growth and logistics efficiency.

items.title: Bankers welcome RBI’s calibrated stance, regulatory steps
items.summary: Bankers have praised the Reserve Bank's decision to maintain current interest rates. This move, alongside specific industry adjustments, is seen as a wise and balanced step. These actions are expected to bolster banks' financial strength and encourage consistent credit expansion. The central bank's focus on macroeconomic stability amid global uncertainties is also noted.

items.title: India's natural diamond market may reach Rs 1.5 lakh cr by 2030: De Beers report
items.summary: The market for natural diamond jewellery in India is on the brink of a remarkable transformation, anticipated to soar to Rs 1,50,000 crore by 2030. This surge is largely attributed to robust economic growth and evolving consumer tastes. Natural diamonds are now being embraced as daily adornments rather than just luxurious splurges for special events.

items.title: India's growth may slow to 6.6% in FY27 due to West Asia war: World Bank
items.summary: The multilateral agency’s estimate is slightly lower than the 6.9% pace projected by the Reserve Bank of (RBI) in its Wednesday's monetary policy statement and the government’s estimate of 6.8-7.2% made in January before the US and Israel began pummelling Iran on 28 February.

items.title: Adani group seeks dismissal of SEC complaint in New York court
items.summary: The matter pertains to proceedings before the United States District Court for the Eastern District of New York, where the SEC has initiated a civil case against the two executives

items.title: Nifty 50 may reclaim record high this fiscal, says HDFC Securities CEO
items.summary: The brokerage remains constructive on Indian equities, with expectations of double-digit earnings growth and relatively stable macroeconomic conditions

items.title: JC Flowers withdraws NCLT plea against Dish TV
items.summary: Dish TV receives a significant relief as JC Flowers Asset Reconstruction withdraws its petition. The petition was filed before the National Company Law Tribunal seeking an extraordinary general meeting. This move comes after a prolonged tussle over board reconstitution. Dish TV shareholders had previously rejected several company proposals. The withdrawal marks a crucial development for the direct-to-home service provider.

items.title: Luxury car sales growth slows to 3% amid global jitters
items.summary: Luxury car sales in India saw a modest 3% rise in the March quarter, with around 14,000 vehicles over Rs 40 lakhs sold. This growth lagged behind the mainstream market, as affluent buyers conserved cash due to geopolitical worries and economic uncertainties. BMW, however, reported a strong 17% increase in its sales.

items.title: Amazon denies reports of 14,000 layoffs planned for May: ‘…not based in fact’
items.summary: Amazon has denied reports that it is planning for another round of mass layoffs in May 2026. The company, rejecting such claims, said these reports are ‘not based on fact’.

items.title: US SEC taps Gibson Dunn attorney to be new enforcement director, sources say
items.summary: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has made a significant announcement with the appointment of David Woodcock as its new enforcement director. Woodcock, who previously steered the SEC's Fort Worth office, takes over from Margaret Ryan, who resigned last month after a six-month stint, citing differences in vision for the enforcement agenda.

items.title: Sebi eases compliance norms for IPO lock-in of pledged shares
items.summary: SEBI rolled out a framework marking pledged shares as “non-transferable” during lock-in, simplifying compliance and enhancing transparency. The regulator also extended IPO observation letter validity amid market challenges.

items.title: Power sector may consume up to 850 mt in FY27; sufficient coal stocks available
items.summary: Coal stocks at TPPs stand at 55.18 mt as on April 6, sufficient for about 24 days based on recent consumption

items.title: Cabinet clears Rs 26,069 crore Kamala hydro project to boost clean power in Arunachal
items.summary: A major hydropower project, the Kamala HEP, has received a significant investment of over Rs 26,000 crore. This 1720 MW project in Arunachal Pradesh will generate clean energy and boost the region's power supply. It will also contribute to flood moderation and socio-economic development. The project is a joint venture and is expected to be completed in 96 months.

items.title: US Stocks: Exxon signals lower Q1 profit despite higher oil, gas revenue from Iran war price spikes
items.summary: ExxonMobil is bracing for a dip in first-quarter earnings, primarily due to a substantial financial hedging loss that is likely to eclipse the benefits of rising oil prices linked to the ongoing Iran conflict. However, as derivative contracts come to fruition, the company expects improved profitability in subsequent quarters.

items.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Thursday
items.summary: Trump’s two-week ceasefire announcement boosted Nifty and global markets, with strong gains across banking, auto, and energy sectors. Technical indicators signal continued bullish momentum, while investors remain cautious of geopolitical risks.

items.title: Market Trading Guide: Buy Hindustan Copper and Hindustan Petroleum on Thursday for gains up to 6%
items.summary: Nifty surged on US President Trump’s two-week pause, supported by banks, auto, and energy sectors. Analysts recommend Hindustan Copper and Hindustan Petroleum for short-term gains with defined stop-loss strategies.

items.title: Cabinet approves Rs 79,459 crore revised cost for HPCL Rajasthan Refinery project
West Asia Conflict: Natural gas allocation to fertiliser sector upped to 95%
items.summary: For comparison, India’s fertiliser sector consumed 9,753 million standard cubic meters (MSCM) of natural gas in H1 FY26, which accounts for 28 per cent of the country’s cumulative usage

items.title: Rs 530 crore block deal: BNP Paribas, SBI MF, others buy stake in Delhivery
items.summary: Delhivery saw heavy institutional activity with BNP Paribas, SBI and Nippon India Mutual Funds buying shares worth over Rs 500 crore. Nexus Ventures sold stakes, while stock gained 4.2%, outperforming market benchmarks

items.title: BSE 'Dharmam Char' seeks to promote financial awareness and values
items.summary: BSE Limited on Tuesday, April 7 launched a financial awareness programm called 'Dharmam Char' in India. The event took place in Mumbai and was attended by Jagadguru Shankar Vijayendra Saraswati, Shankaracharya of Sri Kanchi Kamakoti.

items.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold jumps $204/oz; silver gains $5.8 on safe-haven demand after US-Iran ceasefire
items.summary: A temporary ceasefire in the Middle East has led to increased buying of gold and silver, with prices rising due to expectations of lower interest rates. The US dollar's fall also supported these gains, despite both metals still trading significantly lower than earlier this year.

items.title: West Asia Crisis: Govt assures fuel, coal, LPG supply; welcomes US-Iran ceasefire
items.summary: West Asia Crisis: India has assured sufficient coal, LPG and fuel supply despite the ongoing West Asia crisis, citing strong stock positions and coordinated measures. The government also welcomed the US-Iran ceasefire, stressing stability in global energy routes and uninterrupted supply chains.

items.title: Olive Crown Awards 2026: IAA celebrates ads driving social and environmental change
items.summary: Marketing and communications industry body International Advertising Association (IAA) organised the Olive Crown Awards 2026 at Taj Lands End in Mumbai. This marked the 16th edition of the annual awards.

items.title: Indian govt hikes NBS fertilizer subsidy for kharif season by up to 21% to ₹41,533.81 crore
items.summary: While the potash subsidy has been maintained at the same level, the hike in nitrogen is 10% and it is 21% each in phosphorous and sulphur

items.title: Hilton ties up with Royal Orchid Hotels to add 125 hotels
items.summary: Hilton has signed a similar franchising agreement for the Hampton by Hilton brand with Nile Hospitality and the first hotel is set to open at Amritsar this year

items.title: BEML and DMRC to jointly bid for West Asia metro projects
items.summary: BEML and DMRC are joining forces for metro projects in West Asia. This partnership will offer complete solutions, from rolling stock supply to project execution. The collaboration aims to leverage BEML's manufacturing strength and DMRC's metro system expertise. They will also explore developing indigenous Tunnel Boring Machines for future infrastructure needs.

items.title: Sensex sees best day in 5 years after ceasefire talks
items.summary: While the Sensex had its best day in over five years on Wednesday and the Nifty rose the most in almost a year, experts cautioned that the relief may be temporary, as Tehran has not indicated a definitive pause to its missile and drone strikes.

items.title: SEBI plugs lock-in gap for pledged shares
items.summary: ‘Non-transferable’ tag to ensure compliance with IPO lock-in norms under ICDR rules

items.title: Nifty reclaims 24,000 as ceasefire sparks biggest rally in months
items.summary: Nifty closed at 23,997.35, up 873.70 points or 3.78%; Sensex settled at 77,562.90, adding 2,946 points or 3.95%

items.title: Non-Urban region led value growth for FMCG sector in FY 26: Bizom
items.summary: Packaged food segment’s value growth stood at 7.1% and personal care segment’s value growth was pegged at 4.7%

items.title: JFE Engineering Corp invests ₹43 cr in 2 projects of Antony Waste Handling Cell in AP
items.summary: The company has tied up the equity portion and 70-75 per cent of the project cost will be funded through debt

items.title: RBI proposes easing capital adequacy requirements for banks
items.summary: The move is expected to provide banks greater headroom in maintaining regulatory capital ratios

items.title: Farmers in Kashmir voice concerns over inadequate safety nets as hailstorms devastate apple orchards
items.summary: Compensation currently provided to affected growers ranges between ₹800 and ₹1,000 rupees per kanal, which farmers said say is far below the actual damage incurred

items.title: India welcomes ceasefire in West Asia; seeks ’unimpeded’ navigation
items.summary: Seventeen stranded India-bound tankers await transit through the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: RBI to simplify onboarding process for MSMEs on TReDS
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is proposing to remove due diligence requirements for MSMEs to join TReDS platforms. This move aims to simplify business operations for micro, small, and medium enterprises. TReDS facilitates financing of trade receivables through a digital network.

items.title: CCEA approves ₹73,000 crores plus equity infusion for HPCL Rajasthan Refinery
items.summary: CCEA also approved two hydro power projects in Northeast, while Cabinet gave nod to second phase of Jaipur Metro

items.title: Corporate governance must empower people, says Arundhati Bhattacharya
